name: rasa.module.ingest
description: Portable human workflow over the kernel's document-ingest, RAG-query, and semantic-memory system (canon SA-029) — sweep files in, list and manage document records, query with citations, remember/recall/forget. The engine lives in the kernel; this module ships the skills, the prompting discipline, and the conventions. The one thing that varies per project — which kernel, which element identity, which paths may be swept — is delegated to a project-owned .claude/ingest-gate.md.

rasa.note: The human-workflow half of the kernel's DocumentManager (canon SA-029; kernel TASK-233..239). The kernel owns the engine end-to-end: /v1/files staging, /v1/documents ingest + CRUD + query, /v1/memories, per-element vector indexes, embedding selection, and the kmem agent tools (memory_store / memory_search / doc_search) auto-injected into every claude turn. This module deliberately wraps NONE of that in new plumbing — it ships (1) skills that drive the HTTP surface for a human, (2) ingest-rules.md, the prompting spine that tells the model when to reach for doc_search/memory_search and what belongs in memory vs documents, and (3) the ingest-gate adapter seam. The three genuinely per-project concerns — kernel URL, element identity + scopes, and which paths /ingest may sweep (with load-bearing excludes so a sweep never vectorizes secrets) — live in the project-owned .claude/ingest-gate.md. Cross-module references (audit-ledger entries via module.tasks) are SOFT: present-if-mounted, graceful-if-absent.

element.description: Element-owned ingest files mirrored into the install target's .claude/ per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md §7. Refreshed on Element upgrade. The ingest-gate is NOT here — it is project-owned (see seed.files). There is no project-owned ledger: the kernel's document store IS the record; /docs reads it live.

files.note: The portable ingest spine: when to ingest vs read directly, documents-vs-memory boundary, namespace conventions, the instruction to use the injected doc_search/memory_search tools unprompted, error-code handling, caps, and the known operational gotchas (persistence window, empty-field omission, vision-fallback prerequisites, dims_mismatch). Element-owned; refreshed on upgrade. References .claude/ingest-gate.md for kernel URL + element + sweep roots — it hardcodes none of them.

Ingest skills: /ingest (stage + ingest + stream progress, per-file isolation), /docs (list records), /doc (show / delete one record), /doc-query (RAG query with citations), /remember /recall /forget (semantic memory), /reingest (version-bump re-ingest). Element-owned; refreshed on upgrade.
Describes what this module ships and how it installs. Fork/author-time doc; not installed into consumer projects.

seed.description: One-time, project-owned files copied at install only (skip-if-exists), per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md §7. These belong to the consuming project after install and are never overwritten on Element upgrade — the project's ingest-gate adapter.

files.note: The per-project ingest adapter — kernel_url, the element identity every call is scoped to, default namespace, memory scope, the roots /ingest may sweep, and the excludes it must refuse (secrets, node_modules, .git — load-bearing: a sweep must never vectorize a .env). Project-owned; the project fills it. Ships with honest defaults + commented examples. Placeholder-free per the documented skip-if-exists policy gap.

Stamped with the Element HEAD commit SHA at init time. Lockfile shape per canon Spec §6 (Connection Contract). When co-installed under a parent that also stamps a lockfile, the parent's is authoritative (single-writer-per-path, Lock L-004); in the kernel pull model each Element gets its own holding folder, so no collision. Substitutes the standard {{ELEMENT_*}} / {{TODAY}} placeholders via bin/init.

scaffold.description: Empty directories created at init by the declarative installer. Nothing to scaffold — the document store lives in the kernel, not in project files.
